claim: Baseline electricity consumption ~9,300,000 kWh/yr (112 kWh/m2/yr), calibrated from 24 months of utility billing data.
source: Utility bill export (demo-derived from ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager-style data request), P1 calibration

claim: Baseline gas (fuel) consumption ~1,900,000 kWh-equivalent/yr (23 kWh/m2/yr), central hydronic furnace + DHW plant.
source: Fuel delivery invoices + utility bill export, P1 calibration

claim: Property GFA 83,000 m2, 1 building, 22 floors, built 2003, Office archetype.
source: Building setup / ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager-style property record, P0

claim: Central plant heater is a hydronic_furnace (installed 2003) served via fan_coil_units off the central hydronic loop -- native Audette equipment-survey mapping, not a fan-coil proxy for a different heater type.
source: Site condition assessment cross-walk (third-party report) + audette-modeling-recipes.md section 5a

claim: Central plant cooler is a 2,200-ton water-cooled chiller installed 2010, approaching end of expected service life around Year 3 of the plan.
source: Site equipment inventory / mechanical condition assessment (P1)

claim: DHW plant is a centrally distributed gas_heater installed 2011.
Peer benchmark median site EUI for comparable Office assets is ~100 kWh/m2/yr.
source: Building Performance Database (BPD), filtered by property type (Office) and comparable climate zone, size class 50,000-100,000 m2

claim: Grid emissions factor of 0.35 kgCO2e/kWh used for baseline and target-year emissions (held flat across the hold period for this demo).
source: eGRID location-based subregion average (demo proxy factor)

claim: Natural gas emissions factor of 0.181 kgCO2e/kWh (HHV basis).
source: EPA emissions factor for natural gas combustion

claim: Metro City Building Performance Standard (BPS): first compliance year 2032, cap 30 kgCO2e/m2/yr, fine rate $268/tCO2e over cap.
source: Fictional ordinance modeled on the structure of common large-building carbon-performance standards -- invented for this demo fixture, not a real jurisdiction filing

claim: Air-to-water air-source heat pump COP 3.2 used for the central plant heater electrification measure; heat-pump water heater COP 3.0 for the DHW electrification measure.
source: Audette custom-plan modeling assumptions, cross-checked against manufacturer-class ASHP/HPWH performance curves for the climate zone

claim: Solar PV virtual net metering (VNM) landlord capture of 80% used for the rooftop solar measure.
source: Standard VNM capture convention (Soapbox reporting standard: solar VNM = 0.80 to landlord)

claim: Exit cap rate 6.0%, discount rate 8.0%, 10-year hold period used for the economics waterfall and IRR.
Utility escalation assumptions of 3%/yr electricity and 4%/yr gas (Soapbox default, confirmed at kickoff).
